chapter_context.summary: Pronounces the burden of Tyre and Sidon, lamenting the destruction of the great maritime merchant city and its seventy-year desolation before future restoration.

outline.text: Howling of Tyre over the Destruction of Her Commerce
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Howling of Tyre over the Destruction of Her Commerce (23:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Sidon and Chittim Destitute of Rest
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Sidon and Chittim Destitute of Rest (23:10-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Tyre's Seventy-Year Desolation and Future Holiness of Gain
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Tyre's Seventy-Year Desolation and Future Holiness of Gain (23:15-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 23, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
